name: Whiplash 3PL Fulfillment API
what_it_does: Whiplash (Ryder subsidiary) omnichannel 3PL fulfillment REST API for e-commerce and direct-to-consumer order fulfillment, inventory management, and retail distribution. Enables AI agents to manage order creation and multi-channel routing for omnichannel fulfillment automation, handle inventory allocation and multi-warehouse distribution for fulfillment optimization, access inbound receiving and purchase order management for warehouse inbound automation, retrieve shipping rate and carrier selection for cost-optimized fulfillment automation, manage retail distribution and compliance labeling for wholesale and retail channel automation, handle return processing and grading for reverse logistics automation, access fulfillment billing and cost reporting for 3PL cost management automation, retrieve real-time inventory levels for multi-channel oversell prevention, manage kitting and assembly services for value-added fulfillment automation, and integrate Whiplash with Shopify, BigCommerce, NetSuite, and EDI retail partners for omnichannel commerce.
use_cases: Fulfilling DTC orders from omnichannel agents
Distributing to retail from wholesale fulfillment agents
Managing returns from reverse logistics agents
not_for: Heavy freight and pallet-in/pallet-out bulk distribution
best_when: A DTC e-commerce brand or omnichannel retailer wanting AI agents to automate order fulfillment, inventory management, retail compliance distribution, returns, and kitting across Whiplash's network of US fulfillment centers.
avoid_when: RYDER ACQUISITION INTEGRATION: Whiplash was acquired by Ryder System (NYSE: R) in January 2022; platform integration into Ryder's broader logistics network may affect API stability and feature roadmap; verify current Whiplash API investment posture within Ryder before building automation. RETAIL COMPLIANCE LABELING ACCURACY FOR WHOLESALE: Automated retail distribution via Whiplash for wholesale orders must apply retailer-specific compliance labels (UPC, GS1-128, SSCC carton labels, ticketing); automated wholesale shipments without retailer-compliant labeling create chargeback and vendor compliance fines from major retailers (Target, Walmart, Amazon vendor). INVENTORY ALLOCATION IN MULTI-CHANNEL OVERSELL RISK: Automated inventory allocation across DTC, wholesale, and marketplace channels via Whiplash API must implement channel-priority rules and safety stock; automated full inventory allocation to single channel without multi-channel buffer creates oversell and unfulfillable orders on other channels.

auth.notes: Whiplash uses API key authentication. REST API with JSON. Los Angeles, California HQ (pre-acquisition). Acquired by Ryder System (NYSE: R) January 2022. Founded 2011 (as Whiplash Commerce). 8 fulfillment centers across US. Omnichannel focus: DTC, wholesale, marketplace. Shopify and BigCommerce certified partner. Kitting and value-added services. Competes with ShipMonk, ShipBob, and Flexe for omnichannel 3PL.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-order and per-unit storage fulfillment fees
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-order fulfillment, pick and pack, storage, receiving, kitting; volume pricing for high-volume DTC; retail distribution pricing separate
pricing.notes: Los Angeles CA / Ryder NYSE:R acquisition 2022. Founded 2011. 8 US fulfillment centers. DTC, wholesale, omnichannel focus. Per-order usage pricing.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; order submission and inventory queries throttled; webhooks for order and tracking events

known_agent_gotchas: RYDER ACQUISITION API EVOLUTION UNCERTAINTY: Whiplash API under Ryder ownership may be integrated into Ryder's broader logistics platform; standalone Whiplash API endpoints may evolve or be deprecated as Ryder platform integration progresses; validate API stability commitment before long-term automation investment

RETAIL VENDOR COMPLIANCE CHARGEBACKS FOR WHOLESALE ORDERS: Automated wholesale retail distribution must configure retailer-specific compliance requirements (ASN, labeling, carton configuration) per trading partner; omission of any compliance element (missing GS1-128 label, incorrect ASN timing) triggers retail vendor chargeback; implement retailer compliance profile validation before automated wholesale shipment

KITTING ASSEMBLY LEAD TIME IN ORDER FULFILLMENT SLA: Automated orders requiring kitting or assembly at Whiplash facilities have extended lead time beyond standard pick-pack; automated same-day or next-day shipping SLAs must exclude kitting orders or configure adequate assembly lead time buffer

Multi-channel inventory allocation priority configuration — automated inventory allocation across DTC (Shopify), wholesale (EDI), and marketplace (Amazon) requires explicit channel priority and safety stock configuration; default Whiplash behavior may not implement channel priority needed for multi-channel balance; configure allocation rules before automated multi-channel order acceptance

EDI retail partner integration complexity — Whiplash retail distribution for major retailers (Target, Walmart, Nordstrom) requires EDI integration for PO receipts, ASN, and invoice; automated retail distribution cannot rely solely on REST API; EDI integration is separate implementation from REST API order management

Return grading disposition workflow — automated returns processing at Whiplash requires pre-configured grading criteria (restock, quarantine, destroy, donate) per SKU and return condition; automated returns without grading disposition rules default to quarantine, requiring manual review; configure disposition rules per product category before automated reverse logistics
agent_readiness.security_notes: Omnichannel 3PL. SOC2. API key. US. Order, inventory, and merchant account data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Omnichannel 3PL. SOC2. API key. US only. Order, inventory, and fulfillment data.
